name: James River Group Holdings, Inc.
business: James River Group Holdings, Inc. owns and operates a group of specialty property and casualty insurance companies focused on underwriting small and middle market casualty risks within the U.S. excess and surplus (E&S) lines market. Substantially all of its business is casualty insurance, and for the year ended December 31, 2025, 96.7% of gross written premiums were derived from casualty insurance. The company also has a Specialty Admitted Insurance segment that primarily writes fronting business where it retains a minority share of risk and seeks to earn fee income.
sellsTo: U.S. excess and surplus lines market

exposures.sentence: James River Group Holdings, Inc. owns and operates a group of specialty property and casualty insurance companies focused on underwriting small and middle market casualty risks within the U.S. excess and surplus (E&S) lines market.
The effect of emerging claim and coverage issues on our business is uncertain and may result in coverage of risks that we did not factor in our policy prices.

We rely on our systems and employees, and those of certain third-party vendors and service providers in conducting our operations, and certain failures, including internal or external fraud, operational errors, systems malfunctions, or cyber-security incidents, could materially adversely affect our operations.

catalysts.event: Domestication of holding company from Bermuda to Delaware
catalysts.sentence: On November 7, 2025, we completed a domestication resulting in our holding company, James River Group Holdings, Ltd., changing its jurisdiction of incorporation from Bermuda to Delaware and changing its name to James River Group Holdings, Inc.

catalysts.event: Completion of sale of JRG Reinsurance Company Ltd.
catalysts.sentence: The sale of JRG Re, which closed on April 16, 2024, resulted in the Company's disposition of its casualty reinsurance business and related assets.

catalysts.event: Execution of Combined Loss Portfolio Transfer and Adverse Development Cover (E&S ADC)
catalysts.sentence: On July 2, 2024, James River entered into a Combined Loss Portfolio Transfer and Adverse Development Cover Reinsurance Contract (the E&S ADC) with State National Insurance Company, Inc.

catalysts.event: Execution of E&S Top Up Adverse Development Cover with Enstar
catalysts.sentence: On November 11, 2024, Enstar Group Limited (Enstar), through its subsidiary Cavello Bay Reinsurance Limited (Cavello Bay), entered into an adverse development cover agreement with James River (E&S Top Up ADC).

catalysts.event: A.M. Best affirmation of financial strength rating
catalysts.sentence: On January 30, 2025, A.M. Best affirmed our A- (Excellent) financial strength rating.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 84.5% of gross written premiums and 95.9% of net written premiums originated from the E&S lines market for the year ended December 31, 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: For the year ended December 31, 2025, approximately 84.5% of our gross written premiums and 95.9% of our net written premiums originated from the E&S lines market, which we believe puts us among the top publicly traded insurers as ranked by highest concentrations of E&S risk.

tamClaims.claim: Gross written premiums of $1,172.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, we wrote $1,172.3 million of gross written premiums, allocated by segment and underlying market as follows: Gross Written Premiums by Segment Gross Written Premiums Year Ended December 31, 2025 % of Total (in thousands) Excess and Surplus Lines segment $ 963,035 82.1 % Specialty Admitted Insurance segment 209,284 17.9 % $ 1,172,319 100.0 %
